remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Dataiku named a Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader for 5th consecutive year
remarks.content: For the fifth consecutive year, Dataiku has been named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for AI Platforms for Data Science and Machine Learning, once again recognized in the Leader quadrant for both “Completeness of Vision” and “Ability to Execute.”
remarks.contentTranslation: Dataiku连续第五年被评为2026年Gartner ®数据科学和机器学习AI平台魔力象限™的领导者，再次在“愿景的完整性”和“执行能力”的领导者象限中获得认可。
remarks.translation: Dataiku连续第五年被评为Gartner魔力象限领导者
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: The AI success gap: why more AI doesn’t add up to more value
remarks.content: If AI is everywhere, why are so few companies getting real value from it?That is the question most enterprises are now facing. Eighty-eight percent of companies use AI, but only six percent generate meaningful value from it, according to a study from McKinsey.Pilots run, demos impress, a
remarks.contentTranslation: 如果AI无处不在，为什么很少有公司从中获得真正的价值？这是大多数企业现在面临的问题。根据麦肯锡的一项研究，百分之八十八的公司使用AI，但只有百分之六的公司从中产生有意义的价值。
remarks.translation: AI成功差距：为什么更多的AI不能带来更多的价值

remarks.name: Nikunj Kothari
remarks.role: partner fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire meter, opendoor, atlassian & others. love shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
remarks.text:
The World Cup put on an absolute show today..

Brazil nicking it at the last minute after being 1-0 down and a valiant display by Japan. 

Paraguay deservedly winning against Germany after a WILD penalty shootout. 

Netherlands sneaking into extra time with a 91st minute equalizer against Morocco and this one is probably going to the shootouts too.
remarks.translation: 世界杯今天进行了一场绝对的比赛。巴西队以1比0落后，在日本的英勇表现之后，在最后一分钟获得了这场比赛。在一场疯狂的点球大战后，巴拉圭当之无愧地赢得了对德国的胜利。荷兰队在第91分钟对阵摩洛哥的比赛中偷偷溜进了额外的时间，这场比赛也可能会参加枪战。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Thariq
remarks.role: Claude Code anthropicai.   prev YC W20, southpkcommons, medialab
remarks.text: my process for writing right now is to do some engineering work, talk to a bunch of people about it, brainstorm and research with Claude, write a post, give 1 or 2 talks on it, rewrite the post, give another talk, rewrite the intro, wake up at 6am and rewrite it again, then post
remarks.translation: 我现在写作的过程是做一些工程工作，与一群人谈论它，与克劳德一起头脑风暴和研究，写一篇文章，做一两次演讲，重写文章，再做一次演讲，重写简介，早上6点起床，然后再次重写，然后发布
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.text:
I built a Chrome extension that turns your "read later" list into dedicated reading time on your calendar.  

Save 5 articles → it auto-books a 30-min "reading block" on your Google calendar, links included. So you'll actually sit down and go through them.  

No account, no server, everything local. Open source (link below)
remarks.translation: 我构建了一个Chrome扩展程序，将您的“稍后阅读”列表转换为日历上的专用阅读时间。保存5篇文章→，它会在您的Google日历上自动预订30分钟的“阅读块” ，包括链接。因此，您实际上会坐下来浏览它们。没有账号，没有服务器，一切都是本地的。开源（链接如下）
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Thibault Sottiaux
remarks.role: Codex & ChatGPT OpenAI
remarks.text:
Codex usage limits will be fully reset again in the next hour and we will credit one additional reset into your bank for your own usage over the next 24 hours.

We investigated reports that Codex usage was being consumed faster than expected. There wasn't one central issue, but a few smaller problems compounded for some users.   

Here's what we found and changed: 
- Actual usage: Auto-review had become more proactive, another change was triggering more subagent work, and background suggestions could run twice or retry too frequently after failures. We reverted the changes and fixed suggestion scheduling, duplicate generation, and retry behavior. This should reduce unnecessary background token consumption while preserving the work users explicitly request. 
- Usage reporting: Auto-review was incorrectly appearing as GPT‑5.4 usage, and failed or rate-limited requests were still shown as turns. Auto-review now appears as its own category, and only successful requests count toward the turn graphs. Rate-limited requests were never charged, but they were being displayed incorrectly. 
- Immediate relief: We reset usage limits while rolling out the fixes, then shipped hotfixes across the CLI, desktop app, and usage backend. 
- What to expect: New usage data should be clearer and actual consumption should be lower. Historical charts may still show auto-review under GPT‑5.4 because older turn data was not relabeled. Features that intentionally perform more work; such as /goal, subagents, and higher reasoning levels will still naturally use more capacity.  

All fixes are now deployed, and we've added more detailed monitoring so we can detect background-usage regressions sooner. We'll continue watching the results closely.

Thank you for building and doing all sorts of things with Codex.
remarks.translation:
Codex使用限制将在接下来的一小时内再次完全重置，我们将在接下来的24小时内将额外的一次重置存入您的银行，供您自己使用。我们调查了有关Codex使用速度快于预期的报告。没有一个中心问题，但对于一些用户来说，一些较小的问题变得更加复杂。
以下是我们发现和更改的内容： -实际使用情况：自动审核变得更加积极主动，另一项更改触发了更多的子Agent工作，并且后台建议可能会在失败后运行两次或过于频繁地重试。我们还原了更改并修复了建议计划、重复生成和重试行为。这将减少不必要的后台令牌消耗，同时保留用户明确请求的工作。
-使用情况报告：自动审核错误地显示为GPT ‑ 5.4使用情况，失败或速率受限的请求仍显示为轮次。自动审核现在显示为自己的类别，只有成功的请求才会计入转弯图。我们从未向受速率限制的派单收费，但派单显示不正确。-立即缓解：我们在推出修复程序时重置使用限制，然后在CLI、桌面应用程序和使用后端上发布热修复程序。
-预期：新的使用数据应该更清晰，实际消耗应该更低。由于旧的TURN数据未重新标记，历史图表可能仍会显示GPT ‑ 5.4下的自动审核。有意执行更多工作的功能，如/goal、subagents和较高的推理级别，自然会使用更多容量。所有改进项现已部署完毕，我们添加了更详细的监控功能，以便更快地检测到后台使用情况的退化。
我们将继续密切关注调查结果。感谢您使用Codex构建和完成各种工作。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
remarks.text:
For writing and editing, plain vanilla Claude web is still the best (vs Codex and Claude Code).

My guess is something in the coding agent's system prompts make them crappier writers.
remarks.translation: 对于写作和编辑，纯香草Claude web仍然是最好的（与Codex和Claude Code相比）。我觉得是编码Agent的系统提示使他们成为更蹩脚的作家。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.text:
"The market value of writing has gone way up" (per a PM at Anthropic)

Good writing/clear articulation is required for both building a product (being able to steer models effectively) and building an audience

Writing well has historically been undervalued in tech. Time to change that
remarks.translation: “写作的市场价值已经大幅上升” （根据Anthropic的一位项目经理的说法）构建产品（能够有效地引导模型）和构建受众都需要良好的写作/清晰的表达能力写得好在科技领域一直被低估。是时候改变这一点了
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.text: Count the number of Safari tabs
remarks.content: Tiniest TIL, using AppleScript to count the number of open browser tabs in Safari: osascript -e 'tell application "Safari" to count tabs of every window'
remarks.contentTranslation: 最小的TIL ，使用AppleScript计算Safari中打开的浏览器选项卡的数量： osascript -e '告诉应用程序“Safari”计算每个窗口的选项卡' < img alt = "我在终端窗口中运行它，然后返回370。" src = "
remarks.translation: 统计Safari标签页的数量

remarks.role: Claude is an AI assistant built by anthropicai to be safe, accurate, and secure. Talk to Claude on https://t.co/ZhTwG8d1e5 or download the app.
remarks.text:
Inference runs on Azure infrastructure, operated by Anthropic. Prompt caching and extended thinking are supported today, with more capabilities on the way.

Read more: https://t.co/9MILqQYun4

remarks.name: Boris Cherny
remarks.role: Claude Code anthropicai
remarks.text:
In the next version of Claude Code: subagents run in the background by default, so you can keep talking to Claude while your subagents work

If you want your agent to run in the foreground, just tell Claude
remarks.translation: 在下一个版本的Claude Code中：默认情况下，子Agent在后台运行，因此您可以在子Agent工作时继续与Claude交谈如果您希望Agent在前台运行，只需告诉Claude
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Prev: Product Leader at Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text:
What’s underappreciated is that the rise of strong open-weight models like GLM will actually strengthen Google’s position.

more companies will now start experimenting with fine tuning open-weight models such as GLM and the value will accrue to the infra. 

enterprises want the flexibility to run and fine-tune open models on a managed platform with enterprise-grade reliability, security, and support. And Google Cloud is well positioned there.

also don’t forget Google owns much of the compute stack.
remarks.translation:
低估的是，像GLM这样强大的开放权重模型的崛起实际上将加强谷歌的地位。更多的公司现在将开始尝试微调开放权重模型，如GLM ，其价值将累积到基础设施中。企业希望在具有企业级可靠性、安全性和支持的托管平台上灵活运行和微调开放模型。谷歌云在这方面处于有利地位。
别忘了Google拥有大部分的计算堆栈。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Thibault Sottiaux
remarks.role: Codex & ChatGPT OpenAI
remarks.text:
Advanced Codex users. We shipped a replacement to coarse sandbox modes: reusable, inheritable permission profiles binding OS-enforced file read/write/deny rules (even **/*.env) to per-domain network + Unix sockets. Plus fail-closed admin allowlists. Least privilege per task.

https://t.co/jHyAnUhyFs
remarks.translation: 高级Codex用户。我们提供了粗沙箱模式的替代品：可重用、可继承的权限配置文件将操作系统强制的文件读/写/拒绝规则（甚至* */* .env ）绑定到每个域网络+ Unix套接字。加上失败关闭的管理员允许列表。每个任务的最低权限。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

ok for context this is non-lab workshops at 9am on a monday

theres a competing @OpenAI workshop going on next door but here are the @snyksec , @Atlassian, @neo4j, @arizeai, @Akamai, @togethercompute rooms concurrently. PEOPLE ARE HUNGRY FOR THIS.

https://t.co/qWZMfhe1OW https://t.co/pqTWTlFvqQ

remarks.content: In the last few months, I've started to see [job applications] that were clearly cowritten by an LLM, link to an LLM-generated portfolio site, which then links to LLM-generated GitHub projects, with purely LLM-generated
